Byte-stability regression test: identical fold state → byte-identical renderStatus across wall-clock change
SessionStart source=resume detection: last-event age + transcript size estimate (read-side, best-effort)
Advisory line in injected block + tests (never blocks, never errors)

Implement nudge behind the SPEC + tests

payload.chose: Cycle scope = the memo's three vetted plays (cold-resume advisory, rent-meter with cache-read health bands, batch-complete nudge behind SPEC) plus a byte-stability regression test as the residue of the report's cache-anchoring play 1a
payload.over: Building cache-anchoring as new work, or adopting report Tier-2 (model-tiered/batch bookkeeping, delta injection)
payload.because: The 1a property already exists (renderStatus embeds no volatile bytes) — pin it against silent regression rather than build what's already true. Report Stage-1 hardening confirmed already shipped (independent confirmation the engine was built right, not wasted research). User-approved Jul 12.

payload.chose: Any future cache-cost play must cite token-optimization's rejected approach "leading with prompt caching (doesn't reduce occupancy)" as an informed re-test — legitimate because the scope differs (dollar cost vs token occupancy)
payload.over: Treating the recorded rejection as blocking, or ignoring it as out-of-scope
payload.because: The M4 convention is the M4 convention: informed re-tests that cite the recorded decision are legitimate; silent re-proposal is not. The record governing its own evolution is the product working on itself. User ruling Jul 12.

payload.chose: "sofar makes zero model API calls" promoted to a named architectural invariant (docs/SPEC.md + CLAUDE.md guard-rails); report Tier-2 (Haiku/Batch bookkeeping, 2a/2b) deferred entirely
payload.over: Adopting model-tiered or Batch-API bookkeeping, or leaving the property implicit as an accident of implementation
payload.because: No API keys, no inference costs, no user content sent anywhere; write-backs are already minimal by construction because they are the agent's own tool-call args (report 2c true by construction). Trading that for the measured ~$3.87/2-day upside would be a terrible exchange. User ruling Jul 12.

payload.chose: sofar statusline (3.1 SPEC-lite): a read-side CLI subcommand for Claude Code's statusLine slot — reads statusline JSON from stdin, prints ONE plain line "slug done/total · $cost · cache N%[⚠|✓] · ctx N%", omits any segment it cannot compute, appends nothing to the record, always exits 0. Health marker judged only after 10k tokens: <30% warm ⚠ (prefix nondeterminism), ≥50% ✓. Root resolution: --root/cwd, falling back to the JSON's workspace.current_dir then cwd
payload.over: Auto-installing the statusLine in sofar init (clobbers user config — README documents the one-liner instead), ui-kernel/ANSI styling (statusline is a contract-byte surface, keep plain), or serving cost via the localhost state server (statusline stdin already carries cost + cache tokens at zero API cost)
payload.because: The rent-meter must satisfy the zero-model-API invariant: pure stdin→stdout derivation, no keys, no calls. A statusline must never break or mutate — hooks' best-effort philosophy (BD22) applies verbatim. Bands from the Jul-12 research: healthy stable-prefix workloads run 50–80% cache-read; below 30% signals prefix non-determinism — itself a sofar self-diagnostic.

grep -rn "session-end.sh" packages/engine/test/ packages/engine/src/cli/ | grep -v node_modules | head

payload.chose: Batch-complete nudge (4.1 SPEC): a fifth shim, user-prompt-submit.sh → `sofar event user-prompt` (UserPromptSubmit). When the session is registered AND initiative drift since the last write-back ≥5 mechanical events, emit ONE line of additionalContext nudging sofar_end_session while context is warm; else silence. Stateless and re-fires each prompt until write-back (staleness-line precedent). Mid-session repeat write-backs are blessed: fold is already last-wins (ended/summary/next_action overwritten, freshness resets, Stop passes once any session_ended exists)
payload.over: Nudging via the Stop hook only (too late — the forced block costs an extra cold turn, $0.65/block measured), a stateful once-per-batch memory (violates read-side derivation), or nudging unregistered sessions (noise in unbound/foreign repos)
payload.because: UserPromptSubmit stdout at exit 0 becomes additionalContext that lands AFTER the cached prefix (cache-safe, per the Jul-12 research); write-backs that happen in-flow while context is warm make the Stop gate a fallback instead of a tax. Best-effort per BD22: any failure is silence, never a blocked prompt.

payload.note: SPEC decision D5 logged: UserPromptSubmit shim, drift≥5 threshold, stateless re-fire, repeat write-backs blessed (fold already last-wins).

payload.chose: Restore the default-statusline information INSIDE sofar statusline (D4 amendment): prepend model display name and dir:branch segments — model.display_name from stdin JSON; dir = basename of workspace.current_dir (cwd fallback); branch read from .git/HEAD by bounded upward walk (gitdir-file/worktree aware), no subprocess. Order: model · dir:branch · record · $ · cache · ctx; same independence + omission rules
payload.over: A wrapper script composing sofar's output with hand-rolled default segments (pushes maintenance onto every user), or documenting the loss as intended (the rent-meter should never cost the user the line they had)
payload.because: Claude Code renders exactly one statusLine command — configuring sofar's replaced the default line and its model/dir/branch info (user report, Jul 12). All three segments derive from the same stdin JSON plus one local file read: zero API cost, zero subprocess, best-effort like every other segment.

payload.chose: Styled statusline (D7): the statusline is a HUMAN surface whose consumer (Claude Code's status bar) renders ANSI + emoji even though stdout is piped — so the command wiring FORCES styled caps (bold model, 📁 dir 🌿 branch, accent slug, band-colored cache, threshold-colored ctx ≥70% warn / ≥90% error, dim separators) instead of TTY detection; --no-color or NO_COLOR env falls back to the plain line, byte-identical to 0.8.0. Icons gate on the unicode cap, colors on the color cap; runStatusline's library default stays plain
payload.over: TTY-detected caps (piped output would always strip styles — the one wrong answer for a status bar), emoji-free ANSI only (user asked for icons), or a separate styled variant command
payload.because: User feedback on 0.8.0: the line renders as bare basics. The plain-surface guard protects AGENT surfaces; sofar status/list/doctor already style human surfaces via the ui kernel — the statusline belongs with them, and the kernel costs zero new dependencies (cli-ui D1/D2).

payload.chose: Task-progress pie on the statusline record segment (D9, user feedback): `<pie> <slug> <done>/<total>` with the next.ts coloring convention — success when complete, warn in progress, dim untouched; glyph mode only (plain line stays byte-compatible). plan.goal: Make harness cost felt at the moment it is incurred — cold-resume advisory, statusline rent-meter with cache-read health bands, batch-complete end-session nudge (behind SPEC) — and pin the injection's byte-stability against regression. Governed by the zero-model-API-calls invariant: no play may add a model call, API key, or inference cost to sofar.

payload.chose: Statusline ctx gauge keeps its text label in every mode — `ctx N%` (D10 word-over-glyph extended to the context segment, user feedback); healthy band (<70%) tones success-green instead of dim; model name toned by family inside the D1 semantic palette: Fable bold accent, Opus accent, Sonnet info, Haiku success, unknown families keep the D6 bold
payload.over: The D8 progress-pie ctx gauge with a dim healthy band, and the uniform bold model segment of D6/D7
payload.because: Same ruling as D10: the word carries the meaning — the pie next to a bare percent read as decoration, not a gauge. Color-coding the two most-glanced segments (which model, how full) makes them legible without reading; every tone stays ANSI-16 semantic so the terminal theme supplies the palette. Plain (--no-color) line unchanged, still byte-identical to 0.8.0.
